title: Bulk Claim, Verify, and Reject Operations

story.i_want_to: claim, verify, or reject multiple stories in a single API call
story.so_that: I can efficiently manage batches of stories without making N individual requests, reducing latency and enabling atomic batch workflows

rationale: When an orchestrator verifies stories after an agent completes a batch of work, or when an agent claims multiple ready stories for parallel implementation, issuing individual requests is slow and creates unnecessary latency. Bulk endpoints wrap multiple operations in a single request, processing each story independently with partial-success semantics, while returning per-story results for diagnostics.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim accepts a JSON body with {story_ids: [uuid, ...]} and an agent role API key, attempts to set agent_status=assigned on each specified story independently within a single database transaction using savepoints for partial-success isolation

POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify accepts a JSON body with {stories: [{story_id, result, summary, findings}]} and an orchestrator role API key, attempts to set verified_status=verified on each specified story independently within a single database transaction using savepoints for partial-success isolation

POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/reject accepts a JSON body with {stories: [{story_id, reason, findings}]} and an orchestrator role API key, attempts to set verified_status=rejected on each specified story and auto-reset agent_status to pending independently within a single database transaction using savepoints for partial-success isolation

Bulk operations use PARTIAL-SUCCESS semantics. Each story in the batch is processed independently within the overall transaction. Stories that fail precondition checks are skipped (not rolled back). The response includes per-story results: [{story_id, status: 'success'}, {story_id, status: 'error', reason: '...'}]. The overall HTTP response is 200 if at least one story succeeded, 422 if all failed. Stories that succeed are committed; stories that fail are individually rolled back using Ecto.Multi savepoints.
Bulk claim validates each story individually: story must have agent_status=pending, all story_dependencies must have verified_status=verified, and all parent epic_dependencies must be satisfied
Bulk verify validates each story individually: story must have agent_status=reported_done before it can be verified
Bulk reject validates each story individually: story must have agent_status=reported_done before it can be rejected, and each rejection entry must include a non-empty reason
All bulk operations create individual audit_log entries for each story affected, recording the actor, previous state, new state, and timestamp
All bulk operations fire individual webhook events for each story affected (story.status_changed, story.verified, or story.rejected) if the tenant has webhooks configured

test_cases.name: Bulk claim succeeds for multiple ready stories
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [story1.id, story2.id, story3.id]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response status is 200
Response body contains results array with 3 entries, each with status: 'success'
All 3 stories now have agent_status=assigned and assigned_agent_id=agent.id
3 audit_log entries are created with action='status_changed'

test_cases.name: Bulk claim rolls back when one story fails precondition
preconditions: fixture(:story, %{epic_id: epic.id, agent_status: :assigned}) as story2 (already assigned)
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [story1.id, story2.id, story3.id]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response status is 200 (partial success: story1 and story3 succeeded)
Response body contains per-story results: story1=success, story2=error (already assigned), story3=success
story1 and story3 have agent_status=assigned (succeeded independently)
story2 remains agent_status=assigned with original agent (its savepoint rolled back)
2 audit_log entries created for story1 and story3

test_cases.name: Bulk verify succeeds for multiple reported_done stories
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify with {stories: [{story_id: story1.id, result: 'pass', summary: 'All checks pass'}, {story_id: story2.id, result: 'pass', summary: 'Verified'}]} using orchestrator API key
expected_results: Response status is 200
Response body contains results array with 2 entries, each with status: 'success'
Both stories now have verified_status=verified
2 verification_results records are created
2 audit_log entries are created with action='verified'

test_cases.name: Bulk reject succeeds and auto-resets agent_status
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/reject with {stories: [{story_id: story1.id, reason: 'Missing tests', findings: []}, {story_id: story2.id, reason: 'No migration', findings: []}]} using orchestrator API key
expected_results: Response status is 200
Both stories now have verified_status=rejected
Both stories now have agent_status=pending (auto-reset)
Both stories have rejection_reason populated
2 verification_results records are created with result='fail'
Audit log entries record both the rejection and the auto-reset

test_cases.name: Bulk verify rejects request from agent role
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify with {stories: [{story_id: story.id, result: 'pass', summary: 'ok'}]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response status is 403
Response body contains error indicating insufficient role
Story verified_status remains unchanged

test_cases.name: Bulk claim respects story dependency constraints
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [story1.id, story2.id]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response status is 200 (partial success: story1 succeeded)
Per-story results show story1=success, story2=error because its dependency (story1) is not yet verified
story1 is claimed (agent_status=assigned), story2 remains pending (its savepoint rolled back)

test_cases.name: Bulk reject with missing reason returns validation error
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/reject with {stories: [{story_id: story.id, reason: '', findings: []}]} using orchestrator API key
expected_results: Response status is 422
Response body contains validation error indicating reason is required
Story remains unchanged

test_cases.name: Bulk operations fire webhook events per story
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify with 2 stories using orchestrator API key
expected_results: Response status is 200
2 webhook_events records are created, one per story, with event_type='story.verified'
Each webhook_event has the correct story_id in its payload

test_cases.name: cross-tenant isolation — bulk operations cannot access other tenant's stories
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [tenant_b_story.id]} using Tenant A's agent API key
expected_results: Response indicates failure (story not found or not accessible)
Tenant B's story remains unchanged (RLS prevents cross-tenant access)

technical_notes: Use Ecto.Multi with named steps per story. On individual story failure, use Multi.run/5 that returns {:error, reason} for that story, causing its savepoint to roll back while other stories proceed. ORDER story locks BY id ASC to prevent deadlocks (adversarial review finding).

Validate each story individually within the Multi pipeline; collect all validation errors and return them in the per-story results. Stories that pass are committed; stories that fail are individually rolled back via savepoints.
Lock stories with SELECT FOR UPDATE within the transaction to prevent concurrent claim races. MUST lock in story id ASC order to avoid deadlocks between concurrent bulk operations.
Reuse existing story status transition logic from the single-story endpoints (claim, verify, reject) to ensure consistent validation
Webhook events MUST be created inside the Ecto.Multi transaction for atomicity (consistent with US-10.2 AC-10.2.2). Events are enqueued for delivery after commit via Oban.
Limit batch size to a configurable maximum (default 50) to prevent oversized transactions
The controller should delegate to a BulkOperations context module that coordinates the Ecto.Multi and returns {:ok, results} | {:error, failed_results}

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
